headline: South Florida's tech momentum keeps accelerating with crypto relocations, defense deals, and quantum investments

stories.title: Palantir Sets Up Miami HQ, Crypto Firms Follow Suit
stories.summary: Palantir officially moved its headquarters to the Gold Coast this week, signaling major defense-tech confidence in South Florida. Meanwhile, Elektron Energy (crypto infrastructure) is relocating its US HQ to Brickell.
stories.takeaway: Defense and Web3 companies lowkey making Miami the place to be for next-gen tech

stories.title: Palm Beach State College Lands $5M Quantum Computing Grant
stories.summary: PBSC secured $5M in quantum funding to build a Quantum Innovation Center in downtown West Palm Beach, aiming to attract startups and emerging companies in advanced computing and security.
stories.takeaway: South Florida's locking in serious deep-tech infrastructure—workforce training + quantum talent pipeline hitting different

stories.title: Miami Angels & New World Angels Merge to Create Statewide Network
stories.summary: Two major angel investor groups are combining forces to form a unified statewide network, expanding access to early-stage capital across Florida. The move signals growing maturity in the region's startup financing ecosystem.
stories.takeaway: More organized angel capital = better odds for founders getting seed checks without needing West Coast connections

stories.title: Padel Startup Raises $9M on U.S. Sports Expansion Bet
stories.summary: A Miami startup is betting $9M on padel's explosive U.S. growth, targeting the sport's booming popularity among younger demographics. The play capitalizes on paddle sports becoming mainstream athletic infrastructure.
stories.takeaway: Sports + tech intersection is fr underrated—this startup's riding a real wave (literally)

watchNext.title: Israel Tech Week Miami 2026 (Deadline: March 15)
watchNext.why: Israeli founders can pitch to global investors + media. South Florida already attracting international deep-tech talent, so this is a major sourcing event for the local ecosystem

watchNext.title: eMerge AI Hackathon (April 18)
watchNext.why: Judges from Meta, Lightship Capital, and Haize Labs already confirmed. Building AI products + getting in front of tier-1 investors in one day hits different for makers
